channel.title: NYT > Most Recent Headlines
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026 The New York Times Company

item.title: House Votes to Extend Expiring Law on Warrantless Surveillance for 10 Days
item.description: The Senate would need to also approve the stopgap measure that passed the House early Friday. Libertarian-leaning House Republicans had balked at a long-term extension.
item.dc:creator: Charlie Savage
item.category: Wiretapping and Other Eavesdropping Devices and Methods

media:content.media:description: Gen. Joshua M. Rudd, the director of the National Security Agency, during his confirmation hearing in January.

item.title: Con ustedes, el sándwich de fruta
item.description: En esta delicia japonesa, las joyas maduras de temporada se envuelven en crema y se colocan entre rebanadas de pan de leche.
item.dc:creator: Ligaya Mishan

media:content.media:credit: Chris Simpson para The New York Times. Estilista de alimentos: Maggie Ruggiero. Estilista de atrezzo: Sophia Pappas.

item.title: NYT Spelling Bee Hints for April 17, 2026
item.description: Feeling stuck on today’s puzzle? We can help.
item.dc:creator: New York Times Games

item.title: Two Dogs
item.description: Tell us a story, real or made up, that is inspired by this image.
item.dc:creator: The Learning Network

item.title: Iran War Forces America’s Friends in Asia to Court Its Rivals
item.description: The severe disruption of oil supplies from the Middle East as a result of the war has prompted South Korea and the Philippines to make deals with Russia and Iran.
item.dc:creator: Sui-Lee Wee, Javier C. Hernández, Choe Sang-Hun and Alex Travelli
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
Far East, South and Southeast Asia and Pacific Areas

media:content.media:credit: Ezra Acayan for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: A gas station in Manila. In the Philippines, America’s oldest treaty ally in Asia, the oil shortage prompted officials to declare a national emergency.

item.title: Live Updates: Thousands of Lebanese Try to Head Home After Israel-Lebanon Truce
item.description: The cease-fire removes a major obstacle to U.S.-Iran peace talks. Hezbollah, the Iran-backed militia Israeli forces are targeting in Lebanon, did not say whether it would accept the truce.
item.dc:creator: The New York Times

item.title: Hope and Caution as Lebanon-Israel Cease-Fire Begins
item.description: Celebrations broke out in Lebanon after its government agreed to a 10-day cease-fire with Israel. In both Israel and Lebanon, some people remained wary that the truce would hold.
item.dc:creator: Shawn Paik

item.title: Palabra del día: ‘emulate’
item.description: Esta palabra ha aparecido en 406 artículos en NYTimes.com en el último año. ¿Puedes usarla en una frase?
item.dc:creator: The Learning Network

item.title: Stephen Colbert Delights in Trump’s ‘Ongoing Papal Feud’
item.description: The host of “The Late Show” recapped what he called “yet another day when the entire world is on edge over President Trump’s senseless and elective war — with the pope.”
item.dc:creator: Trish Bendix
item.category: Late Show with Stephen Colbert (TV Program)
The Daily Show with Jon Stewart (TV Program)

item.title: Word of the Day: emulate
item.description: This word has appeared in 406 articles on NYTimes.com in the past year. Can you use it in a sentence?
item.dc:creator: The Learning Network

item.title: A Paris Court Just Rewrote the Rules of Corporate Morality
item.description: The profit motive was on trial. The verdict was scathing.
item.dc:creator: M. Gessen
item.category: War Crimes, Genocide and Crimes Against Humanity
Islamic State in Iraq and Syria (ISIS)

media:content.media:credit: Photo illustration by The New York Times; photographs by Behrouz Mehri and Delil Souleiman/Agence France-Presse — Getty Images
media:content.media:description: Bruno Lafont, the former chief executive of Lafarge.

item.title: El ‘Arco del Triunfo’ de Trump suscita críticas
item.description: La historia de cómo el presidente Trump cuadruplicó el tamaño de la propuesta original del arco sigue un patrón ya conocido.
item.dc:creator: Luke Broadwater and Zachary Small

media:content.media:credit: Anna Rose Layden for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Karoline Leavitt, the White House press secretary, showed the latest renderings for a proposed triumphal arch during a briefing on Wednesday.

item.title: Retrato de un Trump bajo presión
item.description: El presidente de EE. UU. arremete contra enemigos, aliados e incluso el papa. Mientras tanto, los republicanos intentan concentrarse en la economía previo a las elecciones intermedias.
item.dc:creator: Katie Rogers
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Tierney L. Cross/The New York Times
media:content.media:description: El presidente Trump en una pelea de la UFC, la semana pasada en Miami.

item.title: Corrections: April 17, 2026
item.description: No corrections appeared in print on Friday, April 17, 2026.

item.title: Before They Met, Their Parents Had Already Known Each Other
item.description: When Emily Wiser first met Griffin Lear, she was excited to tell her father that she was dating a Dartmouth grad like him. Then they shared some history of their own about Mr. Lear’s parents.
item.dc:creator: Jenny Block

item.title: Painting Over My Painful Past
item.description: My father treated me more like a wife than a child. It took me decades to face the truth of what happened.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Hanssen

item.title: A Duet Decades in the Making
item.description: After meeting for a website project in 2020, James Squires and Jacqui Agostinelli bonded over their love of music, forming both a marriage and their musical duo, Sweet Basil.
item.dc:creator: Shivani Vora

item.title: Her Grandmother Told Her ‘Maybe You’ll Marry Him One Day’
item.description: After Ben Sweeney and Lauren Fogel debarked their plane at 3 a.m., he invited her to stay at his parents’ house nearby. Her grandmother was impressed.
item.dc:creator: Rosalie R. Radomsky

item.title: It Took a Persistent Friend and a Dave Matthews Concert to Seal the Deal
item.description: Daniel Martinez Clavijo and Johanna Mele finally met in 2021, thanks to their mutual friend, who had been trying to set them up for almost a decade, and a chance encounter at Madison Square Garden.
item.dc:creator: Shivani Vora

item.title: Quote of the Day: A Huge Russian Barrage Kills 18 in Ukrainian Cities
item.description: Quotation of the Day for Friday, April 17, 2026.

item.title: Trump Promotes ‘No Tax on Tips’ in Las Vegas and Brushes Off Iran War Inflation Concerns
item.description: At an economic event, the president sought to downplay the financial hardship that has followed his war with Iran, saying that “we’re having some fake inflation because of the fuel, the energy prices.”
item.dc:creator: Chris Cameron
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:description: President Trump sought to give an affordability message in speaking Thursday about his tax policies to workers in Las Vegas.

item.title: ‘The Pitt’ Season 2 Finale Recap: Jagged Little Pills
item.description: For all the death and darkness the Pitt crew had to swallow this season, the finale also offered profound moments of hope and renewal.
item.dc:creator: Sean T. Collins

media:content.media:description: Dana (Katherine LaNasa) hoped someone on staff would foster Baby Jane Doe. Has she found a taker?

item.title: ‘Proof’ Review: Ayo Edebiri as a Math Girl, Interrupted
item.description: The actress stars as a haunted genius opposite Don Cheadle as her father in David Auburn’s 2001 drama. This revival, though, exposes the play’s lack of rigor.
item.dc:creator: Helen Shaw

media:content.media:description: Don Cheadle, left, and Ayo Edebiri as father and daughter in the play “Proof” at the Booth Theater in Manhattan.

item.title: NYT Crossword Answers for April 17, 2026
item.description: Rachel Fabi returns to open our solving weekend.
item.dc:creator: Sean McGowan

media:content.media:description: Sink your teeth into this themeless Friday challenge.

item.title: Acting ICE Director Says He Plans to Resign in May
item.description: Todd Lyons said he would leave to spend more time with his family. He has spoken about a surge in threats against ICE officers, saying that he knew the reality firsthand.
item.dc:creator: Madeleine Ngo and Hamed Aleaziz

media:content.media:credit: Eric Lee for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: The exit of Todd Lyons as acting director of Immigration and Customs Enforcement raises more questions about the direction of the agency, which has faced dimming public opinion.

item.title: Nearly Everyone on ‘The Pitt’ Has Trauma
item.description: On top of the daily toll of treating patients, the show’s medical providers bring their own scars to the E.R.
item.dc:creator: Christina Caron

item.title: D4vd Arrested in Connection With Death of Celeste Rivas Hernandez
item.description: The singer was detained in connection with the death of Celeste Rivas Hernandez, who died before her 15th birthday. D4vd’s lawyers said they would vigorously defend his innocence.
item.dc:creator: Matt Stevens and Orlando Mayorquín

media:content.media:description: The body of a teenage girl was found in a car belonging to the singer D4vd.

item.title: Former Navy Service Member Charged in Georgia Killing Spree
item.description: The suspect is accused of carrying out a string of attacks in the Atlanta area that killed two women and injured a homeless man. The authorities described the attacks as random.
item.dc:creator: Pooja Salhotra

media:content.media:credit: Ben Hendren/Atlanta Journal-Constitution, via Associated Press
media:content.media:description: The street where Lauren Bullis, a Department of Homeland Security employee, was killed while walking her dog on Monday in Decatur, Ga.

item.title: F.A.A. Caps Flights at O’Hare Airport in Chicago to Cut Delays
item.description: Set to be in place throughout the summer, the limit aims to ease congestion after less than 60 percent of flights ran on schedule.
item.dc:creator: Mark Walker

media:content.media:credit: Jamie Kelter Davis for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Air traffic around O’Hare International Airport has surged in recent years.

item.title: Israel y Líbano acuerdan un alto al fuego de 10 días
item.description: La pausa en los combates entre las fuerzas israelíes y los militantes de Hizbulá, aliados de Irán, ayudaría a allanar el camino hacia un acuerdo de paz entre EE. UU e Irán.
item.dc:creator: Euan Ward, Isabel Kershner, Eric Schmitt and Thomas Fuller
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Diego Ibarra Sanchez para The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Beirut, Líbano, el jueves. Debido a los combates, cientos de miles de libaneses se han visto obligados a abandonar sus hogares.

item.title: Officials Investigate Bomb Threat at Home of Pope’s Brother
item.description: The police in suburban Chicago said they found no explosive devices. The investigation comes amid a public dispute between the pope and President Trump over the Iran war.
item.dc:creator: Christina Morales

media:content.media:description: The investigation follows days of tension between the pope and President Trump over the Iran war.

item.title: An Arrest in the Streets of New York, on Horseback
item.description: An officer from the Police Department’s Mounted Unit raced through the Upper West Side of Manhattan, chasing a woman accused of snatching a purse.
item.dc:creator: Liam Stack

item.title: Lebanon-Israel Cease-Fire Goes Into Effect
item.description: A 10-day cease-fire went into effect at midnight Thursday in Lebanon. The truce was brokered by the U.S. government and announced by President Trump.
item.dc:creator: Jackeline Luna

item.title: TK
item.description: TK

item.title: Ellison Pledges at Least 30 Paramount-Warner Movies a Year
item.description: His remarks at a movie theater convention were part of a campaign to win Hollywood support for his bid to buy Warner Bros. Discovery.
item.dc:creator: Nicole Sperling

media:content.media:description: David Ellison, the chief executive of Paramount Skydance, speaking at CinemaCon on Thursday.

item.title: Michigan’s Dam Crisis Shows Decay of U.S. Infrastructure, Whitmer Says
item.description: Workers in Cheboygan hurried to shore up a dam in danger of being overtopped, part of a “slow-moving disaster” threatening communities across the state.
item.dc:creator: Billy Witz, Sam Easter and Amy Graff

media:content.media:credit: Jan-Michael Stump/Traverse City Record-Eagle, via Associated Press
media:content.media:description: A team of municipality workers cleared debris from the Boardman River Weir that was blocking the flow of the flooded Boardman/Ottaway River in downtown Traverse City, Mich., on Wednesday.

item.title: Trump to Pick Ousted FEMA Head to Lead Agency Again
item.description: Cameron Hamilton is a former Navy SEAL who ran unsuccessfully for Congress.
item.dc:creator: Tyler Pager, Scott Dance and Zolan Kanno-Youngs

media:content.media:description: Cameron Hamilton told a House committee last May, when he was the acting chief of FEMA, that the agency was vital to communities “in their greatest times of need” and should not be eliminated. He was ousted the next day.

item.title: This Is Where Donald Trump Loses
item.description: President Trump won’t be able to defy the courts if he loses his Supreme Court case on birthright citizenship, which he is likely to do, the conservative court watcher Sarah Isgur argues on “Interesting Times.”
item.dc:creator: Interesting Times

item.title: Democrats Confront RFK Jr. on Vaccines and Health Care Fraud
item.description: Lawmakers confronted Robert F. Kennedy Jr. on vaccine messaging and the Trump administration’s handling of health care fraud. The health secretary was also criticized over comments he made about psychiatric medications and Black children, which he denied.
item.dc:creator: Jorge Mitssunaga

media:content.media:description: Democrats assailed Health Secretary Robert F. Kennedy Jr. over his past statements on health policy.

item.title: The Failure of Donald Trump
item.description: President Trump’s efforts to rule through executive order on issues like tariffs and birthright citizenship have largely failed — and will continue to fail — the conservative court watcher Sarah Isgur argues on “Interesting Times.”
item.dc:creator: Interesting Times

item.title: Israel and Lebanon Agree to a Cease-Fire Deal
item.description: Also, Utah is now the epicenter of U.S. measles cases. Here’s the latest at the end of Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Cullen

media:content.media:description: Residents of central Beirut on Tuesday, near the site of Israeli air strikes.

item.title: Mamdani Says He Plans to Skip the 2026 Met Gala
item.description: In an interview with the local news outfit Hell Gate, the New York mayor framed his decision to avoid the glamorous fund-raiser as a way to keep his focus on affordability.
item.dc:creator: Yola Mzizi

media:content.media:credit: Angelina Katsanis for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Mayor Zohran Mamdani might have seemed out of place at the Met Gala, an event that is widely considered to be the playground of the rich and famous.

item.title: Firefighters Likely Had Limited View of Approaching Plane in LaGuardia Crash
item.description: The Times created a 3-D model to visualize the sight lines from inside the fire truck seconds before it crashed with a passenger jet.
item.dc:creator: Helmuth Rosales, Raj Saha, Elena Shao, Corey Kilgannon and Bedel Saget
item.category: Port Authority of New York and New Jersey

item.title: What Heelys, the Sneakers With Wheels, Taught Me About Momentum as a Child
item.description: Heelys, the sneakers with wheels that were a fad in the 2000s, helped me form my first friendships. Recently, I decided to tap into that joy again.
item.dc:creator: Ash Wu

media:content.media:description: Ash Wu wearing a pair of Heelys. Reconnecting with the shoes brought back memories of her childhood.

item.title: Roger Adams Dies at 71; Invented the Rolling Sneakers Known as Heelys
item.description: You could walk in them like gym shoes, but if you rocked back on your heels the wheels emerged, turning them into roller skates. In the early 2000s, the company sold millions.
item.dc:creator: Richard Sandomir

item.title: 10-Day Cease-Fire Between Israel and Lebanon Goes Into Effect
item.description: The pause in the fighting between Israeli forces and Iranian-allied Hezbollah militants may help smooth the way to a peace deal between the U.S. and Iran.
item.dc:creator: Euan Ward, Isabel Kershner, Eric Schmitt and Thomas Fuller
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Diego Ibarra Sanchez for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Beirut, Lebanon, on Thursday. Hundreds of thousands of Lebanese have been forced by the fighting to leave their homes.

item.title: Pittsburgh’s Children’s Museum Satisfies a Hunger for Eric Carle
item.description: The institution will feature five of the beloved author’s collage-based books in a series of interactive exhibits meant to engage children.
item.dc:creator: Shivani Vora

media:content.media:credit: Ross Mantle for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Preschoolers from Pittsburgh Public Schools hiding in and weaving through a field of four-foot-tall blades of grass in “The Very Clumsy Click Beetle” section of “Very Eric Carle.”

item.title: A ‘Cabinet of Wonders’ on Show at the American Academy of Arts and Letters
item.description: Current members of the museum have created a show that draws from, and comments on, the institution’s curious collections.
item.dc:creator: Laurel Graeber
item.category: American Academy of Arts and Letters

item.title: Bitcoin’s Greatest Mystery
item.description: The creator of Bitcoin has hidden behind the pseudonym Satoshi Nakamoto for 17 years.
item.dc:creator: Katrin Bennhold
